text: The Heidelberg Catechism’s Q&A 45 has been answered in vastly different ways throughout its long history. Using a hermeneutical lens, this article examines several of the historical contexts in which Q&A 45 has been understood, and in so doing, makes a first foray into uncovering the hermeneutics behind Scripture’s interpretation for the HC. The goal of interpreting the Bible extends beyond merely understanding the theology that Bible authors intended to communicate (exegetical level). Rather, this goal also includes uncovering the hermeneutics employed by those interpreters and examining the extent to which those hermeneutics are understandable to biblical readers. Unfortunately, for many lay readers, this level of technical interpretation does not feel relatable and therefore can be very alienating. Using the resurrection of Christ as a doctrine that all of these varying interpretations have in common, this paper demonstrates how very different hermeneutics can yield understandings of doctrine that are dramatically different from one another.
